title: कृष्ण ध्यानम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कृष्ण ध्यानम् ॥

वसुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥ १॥

कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय देवकीनन्दनाय च।
नन्दगोपकुमाराय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः॥ २॥

तमालश्यामलं कृष्णं चन्द्रबिम्बाननं हरिम्।
वंशीवादनशीलं च ध्यायेत्पद्मनिभेक्षणम्॥ ३॥

मेघश्यामं पीतकौशेयवासं
श्रीवत्साङ्कं कौस्तुभोद्भासिताङ्गम्।
गोपीनाथं नन्दगोपप्रियं तं
वन्दे कृष्णं जगदीशं नमामि॥ ४॥

मन्दारगन्धसमवायसुगन्धि
तैलाभिषिक्तमलकं तिलकोज्ज्वलाङ्गम्।
मायाधरं मुरलीधरमाशुतोषं
गोवर्धनोद्धरणमद्भुतबालकृष्णम्॥ ५॥

करारविन्देन पदारविन्दं
मुखारविन्दे विनिवेशयन्तम्।
वटस्य पत्रस्य पुटे शयानं
बालं मुकुन्दं मनसा स्मरामि॥ ६॥

नन्दनन्दनमानन्दकन्दं
परमानन्दं सच्चिदानन्दम्।
राधासहचरं कृष्णं
ध्यायेत्सदा हृदि सन्ततम्॥ ७॥

ॐ क्लीं कृष्णाय गोविन्दाय
गोपीजनवल्लभाय स्वाहा॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री कृष्ण ध्यानम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Krishna Dhyanam ||

Vasudeva Sutam Devam Kamsa Chanura Mardanam |
Devaki Paramanandaam Krishnam Vande Jagadgurum || 1 ||

Krishnaya Vasudevaya Devaki Nandanaya Cha |
Nanda Gopa Kumaraya Govindaya Namo Namah || 2 ||

Tamala Shyamalam Krishnam Chandra Bimba Ananam Harim |
Vamshi Vadana Shilam Cha Dhyayet Padma Nibhekshanam || 3 ||

Meghashyamam Pita Kausheya Vasam
Shrivatsankam Kaustubhodbhasitangam |
Gopinath Nanda Gopa Priyam Tam
Vande Krishnam Jagadisham Namami || 4 ||

Mandara Gandha Samavaya Sugandhi
Tailabhishiktam Alakam Tilako Jjvalangam |
Mayadharam Muralidharam Ashutosham
Govardhana Uddharanam Adbhuta Bala Krishnam || 5 ||

Kararavindena Padaravindam
Mukharavinde Viniveshayantam |
Vatasya Patrasya Pute Shayanam
Balam Mukundam Manasa Smarami || 6 ||

Nanda Nandanam Ananda Kandam
Paramanandaam Sachchidanandam |
Radha Sahacharam Krishnam
Dhyayet Sada Hridi Santatam || 7 ||

Om Klim Krishnaya Govindaya
Gopi Jana Vallabhaya Svaha || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Krishna Dhyanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वासुदेव के पुत्र, कंस-चाणूर को मारने वाले, देवकी के परमानन्द, जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को वन्दन।

कृष्ण, वासुदेव, देवकीनन्दन, नन्दगोपकुमार, गोविन्द को बारम्बार नमस्कार।

तमाल वृक्ष सदृश श्यामल, चन्द्रबिम्ब सदृश मुख, वंशी वादन में तत्पर, कमल सदृश नेत्र वाले कृष्ण का ध्यान करें।

मेघश्याम, पीतवस्त्र, श्रीवत्सचिह्नित, कौस्तुभोद्भासित, गोपीनाथ, नन्दगोपप्रिय कृष्ण जगदीश को वन्दन।

मन्दार पुष्प सुगन्धी तेल से अभिषिक्त अलक, तिलकोज्ज्वल अंग, मायाधर, मुरलीधर, आशुतोष, गोवर्धन उठाने वाले अद्भुत बालकृष्ण।

कमल सदृश हाथ से कमल सदृश पैर को कमल सदृश मुख में रखते हुए, वट पत्र पर शयन करने वाले बाल मुकुन्द को मन से स्मरण करता हूँ।

नन्दनन्दन, आनन्दकन्द, परमानन्द, सच्चिदानन्द, राधासहचर कृष्ण का सदा हृदय में ध्यान करें।
benefits: Complete Krishna meditation collection from childhood to Dwarkadhish
Contains the iconic Bal Mukunda shloka of infant Krishna on Vata leaf
Includes Govardhana leela and Murlidhar meditation
Krishna bija mantra for deep devotional meditation
Ideal for daily Krishna worship, Janmashtami and Ekadashi
description: Krishna Dhyanam — कृष्ण ध्यानम् — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Krishna. Attributed to Bhagavata Purana / Gita Govinda, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to complete Krishna meditation collection from childhood to Dwarkadhish, krishna bija mantra for deep devotional meditation, and contain the iconic Bal Mukunda shloka of infant Krishna on Vata leaf. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Daily and during Janmashtami, Holi, and Gopashtami. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Krishna Dhyanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Krishna Dhyanam meditation shlokas in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Includes Bal Mukunda, Murlidhar and Govardhana meditation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Krishna Dhyanam and what is its purpose?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Dhyanam (कृष्ण ध्यानम्) is a curated collection of meditation (dhyana) shlokas dedicated to Lord Krishna — preserved in classical tradition from sources including the Bhagavata Purana, the Gita Govinda of Jayadeva, and various Vaishnava paddhatis. Dhyana shlokas are the foundational verses recited at the very beginning of any puja, japa or stotra-parayana to fix the visual form (rupa) of the deity in the meditator's mind before any worship can begin. The Krishna Dhyanam visualises Krishna in his complete iconic forms — the dark megha-shyama complexion, the peetambara (yellow silk garment), the Shrivatsa mark on the chest, the Kaustubha jewel, the venu (flute) at his lips, the peacock-feather mukuta on his head, and the divine Radha at his side. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana; Gita Govinda; Vaishnava puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Vasudeva Sutam Devam Kamsa Chanura Mardanam?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated opening shloka of the Krishna Dhyanam declares: I bow to Krishna — the Jagadguru (universal teacher), the divine son (sutam) of Vasudeva, the slayer (mardanam) of the demons Kamsa and Chanura, and the supreme bliss (paramanandam) of mother Devaki. The verse compactly encapsulates Krishna's identity through three of his most defining roles — his divine birth in the Yadu lineage as Vasudeva-putra, his victory over the tyrant uncle Kamsa and the wrestler Chanura at Mathura which liberated his birthplace, and his role as the cosmic Jagadguru who delivered the Bhagavad Gita. It is one of the most universally chanted Krishna shlokas in the Vaishnava tradition. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 10th Skandha; classical Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Bal Mukunda dhyana shloka in the Krishna Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse Karaaravindena padaravindam mukharavinde viniveshayantam, vatasya patrasya pute shayanam balam mukundam manasa smarami is one of the most beloved meditation shlokas in all of Sanskrit devotional literature. It visualises the infant (bala) Krishna lying on a single floating peepal leaf (vatasya patrasya pute) at the time of pralaya (cosmic dissolution), holding his lotus-foot (padaravindam) with his lotus-hand (kararavindam) and placing the toe in his lotus-mouth (mukharavinde) — an image of cosmic innocence containing the entire universe. The verse is the meditation seed (dhyana-bija) for Bala-Mukunda upasana and is recited at every Janmashtami, in every Krishna puja, and during Bhagavata Saptaha. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 12.9; Vishnu Purana; Krishnopanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Klim Krishnaya Govindaya bija mantra used in the dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse Om Klim Krishnaya Govindaya Gopijana-vallabhaya Svaha is the eighteen-syllable Krishna mantra (krishna-ashtadasakshari) — one of the most powerful mantras in the entire Vaishnava and Goudiya tradition. The bija Klim is the kama-bija (the seed of divine love and attraction), traditionally identified as the bija of Krishna himself in his role as the supreme attractor (akarshana). The mantra invokes Krishna in three of his sacred names — Krishna (the all-attractive dark one), Govinda (lord of cows and senses), and Gopijana-vallabha (the beloved of the gopis). It is the primary upadesha-mantra in the Goudiya, Nimbarka and Vallabha sampradayas and is the seed mantra for prema-bhakti (divine love devotion). (Sources: Gopala Tapani Upanishad; Goudiya paddhati; classical Vaishnava agama.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Krishna Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Dhyanam is most auspicious at: (1) Brahma muhurta (the 96 minutes before sunrise) for daily Krishna upasana; (2) every Wednesday (Budhvar — traditionally associated with Krishna and Vishnu); (3) every Ekadashi (the eleventh lunar day sacred to Vishnu-Krishna); (4) Krishna Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — the supreme Krishna festival celebrating his birth); (5) Gopashtami (Kartik Shukla Ashtami — celebrating Krishna's first day as cowherd); (6) Holi (Phalgun Purnima — celebrating Krishna's leelas with Radha and gopis); (7) Govardhan Puja (Kartik Shukla Pratipada); (8) before reciting the Vishnu Sahasranama, Bhagavad Gita parayana, or any Krishna stotra; (9) at the start of any puja as the dhyana-shloka; (10) before commencing meditation or yogic practice. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vaishnava calendar; classical paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to regular Krishna Dhyanam recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following phala to regular meditation on Krishna through the Dhyanam: (1) deepening of bhakti and the awakening of prema (divine love) for Krishna; (2) clearing of the mind and steadying of consciousness through visualisation of the divine form; (3) protection from negative influences, fear and anxiety through Krishna's grace; (4) success in righteous undertakings as Krishna is Yogeshwara; (5) eligibility for Bhagavad Gita study and Bhagavata Purana parayana; (6) removal of accumulated sins through the merit of Krishna-smarana; (7) attainment of Goloka Vrindavan — the supreme abode of Krishna — at the end of life through sustained smarana; (8) eligibility for prema-bhakti and rasa-anubhuti as taught by the Goudiya acharyas. The Bhagavata declares that even one moment of Krishna-dhyana destroys mountains of karma. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana phalashruti; Gita 12; Vaishnava acharyas.)
